- Leaf 7.0: HLE, ERMS, FSRM, CET_IBT

* fix: detect leftward overflow from justify-end for paying users with more nav buttons

The mobile nav uses justify-end, which pushes overflowing items to the
LEFT. But scrollWidth only measures rightward overflow, so it never
detected the overflow for logged-in users (4-5 buttons).

Added a getBoundingClientRect check: if the first nav item is pushed
past the container's left edge, overflow is detected and the logo
swaps to the compact logomarca.

- Logged in not paid (4 buttons): swaps to logomarca, all visible
- Logged in paid (5 buttons): swaps to logomarca, all visible

commit.message:
DX-2429: unify Run API with StreamRun, typed chunks, and private internals (#68)

* feat!: unify Run API with StreamRun, typed chunks, and private internals

Redesign the Run system to separate streaming from non-streaming runs
at the type level, yield typed Chunk objects instead of raw strings,
and encapsulate all internal state behind private fields.

BREAKING CHANGES:

- `Run` no longer implements `AsyncIterable`. Streaming methods now
  return `StreamRun`, a new subclass that implements `AsyncIterable<C>`.
  Code doing `for await (const x of run)` on a non-streaming run will
  no longer compile.

- `agent.stream()` returns `Promise<StreamRun<string, Chunk>>` instead
  of `Promise<Run<string>>`. The iterator now yields `Chunk` objects
  (discriminated union: text-delta, tool-call, reasoning, start, finish,
  stats) instead of plain strings. Consumers must check `chunk.type`
  to extract text: `if (chunk.type === "text-delta") chunk.text`.

- `exec.stream()` and `exec.streamCode()` return
  `Promise<StreamRun<string, ExecStreamChunk>>`. The iterator yields
  `ExecStreamChunk` objects (`{ type: "output", data }` or
  `{ type: "exit", exitCode, cpuNs }`) instead of plain strings.

- `run.status` is now a synchronous getter (returns cached status)
  instead of an async method that polls the backend.

- `run.type` uses `"command"` instead of `"shell"` for exec runs.

- `exec.code()` returns `Run<string>` instead of `CodeExecutionResult`.
  Use `run.result`, `run.exitCode`, `run.status` instead of
  `result.output`, `result.exit_code`.

- All internal `_`-prefixed fields on `Run` and `StreamRun` are now
  private. Use the public getters (`id`, `status`, `result`, `exitCode`,
  `cost`) instead. SDK internals use `Run._update()` and
  `StreamRun._setIterator()` static methods.

- `Box._run()` and `Box._stream()` are now private methods.

Other changes:
- New export: `StreamRun` from `@upstash/box`
- CLI `handleRun` now iterates Chunk objects directly, eliminating the
  pendingEvents queue workaround
- Added missing `await` on rejects assertion in cd integration test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use `run.exitCode` instead of `run.status` for shell exit code checks (#69)

* Initial plan

* fix: use run.exitCode instead of run.status for numeric comparisons in test-isolation.ts

Co-authored-by: CahidArda <57228345+CahidArda@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: CahidArda <57228345+CahidArda@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: finalize StreamRun state on early termination and errors

Add try/catch/finally to all streaming iterators (_stream, _execStream,
_execStreamCode) so that Run state is always updated:

- On error: status set to "failed" with partial result and computeMs
- On early break/return: status set to "detached" (new RunStatus) with
  partial result — distinct from "cancelled" since the run may still be
  executing server-side
- Reader is always cancelled in the finally block

Also adds "detached" to RunStatus and fixes openai-codex example using
private fields.

commit.message:
feat: zero-serialization WAL binary streaming via PipeWriter (#1332) (#1344)

Implement binary length-prefixed WAL streaming endpoint at GET /bmw/wal/stream.

Wire format:
  [count:uint32] repeat: [length:uint32] [wal record bytes...]

- WalStreamWriter.StreamAllAsync: streams entire HeadMap via CopyArrays
- WalStreamWriter.StreamEntityAsync: streams per-entity records via QueryBinary
- Endpoint accepts ?entity=X query param for filtered streaming
- Zero intermediate buffers — WAL payloads written directly to PipeWriter
- Back-pressure via periodic FlushAsync every 64 records
- Records streamed as-is (may be compressed/encrypted per WAL codec)

5 tests covering: empty store, multi-record framing, unknown entity,
cancellation, and large payload correctness.

Closes #1332

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: echo back client message_id in SendMessage response

Server was generating a new UUID unconditionally, breaking delivery receipts.
Per proto contract (SendMessageResponse.message_id comment: 'echo back'),
the server must return the same ID the client sent.

Priority:
  1. envelope.message_id (from Envelope.message_id_type oneof)
  2. request.idempotency_key (if provided and non-empty)
  3. generated UUID (fallback only when client sends no ID)

Fixes:
- Delivery receipts not matching (recipient acks server UUID, sender
  looks up client UUID → never finds it)
- 'Delivered' status never set on sender side
- Potential re-delivery on reconnect due to unresolved ACK

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix black screen when installed in Program Files on Windows (#93)

WebView2 defaults to creating its user data folder next to the executable.
When installed in C:\Program Files\, the process lacks write permissions,
causing WebView2 initialization to fail silently (black screen after splash).

Set the WebView2 user data folder to %LocalAppData%\MauiSherpa\WebView2
so the app works regardless of install location.

Fixes #89

  markdown-vendor: new 140 KB lazy chunk

commit.message:
fix: robust routing — no more 404s on GitHub Pages

Three-pronged routing fix:

1. navigate() always uses hash mode
   Removed broken History/Hash mode detection that sometimes fell into
   pushState mode, causing real path changes that 404 on static hosts.
   Now always does location.set_hash('#/path').

2. 404.html SPA redirect
   GitHub Pages serves this for any unknown path. Redirects
   /Oxide/components/button → /Oxide/#/components/button so
   direct links, bookmarks, and refreshes all work.

3. Proper href attributes on all nav links
   Links now have href='#/path' so right-click → open-in-new-tab
   works correctly. Click handler calls preventDefault + navigate().

4. index.html self-redirect
   Strips /index.html from URL if present, redirecting to clean /.

commit.message:
fix(orleans): R5 final polish — SchedulerGrain error observability gaps

3 critical fixes in SchedulerGrain:
1. RegisterJobAsync: bare catch swallowed original exception — rollback
   logged but root cause (storage/reminder failure) was silently lost,
   making production debugging impossible. Now logs the original error.
2. UnregisterJobAsync: bare catch on WriteStateAsync silently swallowed
   persistence failures — operator had no indication of why unregister
   failed. Now logs specific storage error before rollback.
3. TriggerJobAsync: unguarded WriteStateAsync after successful dispatch
   propagated to ReceiveReminder as generic error — job ran correctly
   but LastRunAt/NextRunAt loss caused potential double-execution on
   next reminder tick. Now catches and logs persistence failure separately.

commit.message:
fix(ml): R5 final polish — feature matrix validation, manifest-model consistency, shape assertions

- Add _validate_feature_matrix() guard to all 4 Python training scripts
  (lstm_training.py, train_qlstm.py, train_qsvm.py, train_lstm.py):
  rejects all-NaN columns (scaler 0/0), too-few features (inference
  shape mismatch), and warns on >50% NaN columns before training starts

- Fix manifest-model phantom entries in SaveModelManifestAsync: filter
  to only uploaded models (modelHashes), verify blob existence before
  saving. Previously quality-gate-rejected assets appeared in manifest
  causing 404 on inference download

- Add X/y shape consistency assertions after prepare_sequences() in
  lstm_training.py and train_lstm.py to catch off-by-one bugs before
  they surface as cryptic DataLoader errors mid-training
